methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Modern Intelligence is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by John Dulin that has raised $5.12M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including adjacent-stage companies such as Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a value proxy and applying a standard 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $13M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M (Tenna Systems floor) to $500M (Stark Defence ceiling). The peer-median method carries material weight given the presence of a direct Seed-stage comparable in Tenna Systems.

The two methods produce a wide spread. Triangulated across both approaches, the analysis supports a valuation range of $11M (floor, stage-progression conservative case) to $259M (ceiling, peer-median high case), with a median implied value of $119M. Key uncertainties include Modern Intelligence's relative progress against Seed-stage milestones compared to Tenna Systems, the company's defensibility and technical differentiation within the crowded defense-tech market, and customer traction to date. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects fundamental uncertainty about whether Modern Intelligence's trajectory and risk profile align with earlier or more mature comparables.
